methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $72M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Lumotive, a Series B semiconductor company founded by Gleb Akselrod, has raised $72M to date. Valuation analysis relies on two applicable methodologies anchored to a peer set of 6 private-sector comparables in Chips & Semiconductors, ranging from Series A through Series C stage (Etched, d-Matrix, Positron AI, Substrate, DEEPX, and one additional peer).

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $137M. Peer-median triangulation across the 6-company comparable set produces $1.0B, with peers distributed across a $500M to $5.0B range. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 20.3x across ALAB, NVDA, AMD) cannot be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure from the target. These two applicable methods imply a valuation range of $116M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $568M.

The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around Lumotive's competitive position and near-term revenue trajectory relative to its peer set. Stage-progression offers a floor anchored to capital deployed; peer medians anchor to comparable stage and sector context. Key unknowns affecting valuation placement within this range include product-market traction, gross margins, and go-to-market velocity relative to the sampled comparables.
